   610→    private func handleUniversalLink(_ url: URL) {
   611→        // Parse token from URL (format: https://souvenir.kerry.ink/s/{token})
   612→        guard let token = shareService.parseToken(from: url) else {
   613→            print("[UniversalLink] Invalid URL format: \(url)")
   614→            return
   615→        }
   616→
   617→        // Validate token and get souvenir
   618→        guard let souvenir = shareService.validateToken(token, in: viewContext) else {
   619→            print("[UniversalLink] Invalid or expired token: \(token)")
   620→            return
   621→        }
   622→
   623→        // Navigate to souvenir focus view
   624→        router.navigateToRoot()
   625→        router.navigate(to: .souvenir(id: souvenir.id))
   626→
   627→        print("[UniversalLink] Opened souvenir: \(souvenir.displayTitle)")
   628→    }

   630→    private func fetchSouvenir(id: UUID) -> SouvenirEntity? {
   631→        let request = SouvenirEntity.fetchRequest()
   632→        request.predicate = NSCompoundPredicate(andPredicateWithSubpredicates: [
   633→            SouvenirEntity.activePredicate,
   634→            NSPredicate(format: "id == %@", id as CVarArg)
   635→        ])
   636→        request.fetchLimit = 1
   637→        return try? viewContext.fetch(request).first
   638→    }
   639→
   640→    private func fetchContact(by id: UUID) -> ContactEntity? {
   641→        let request = ContactEntity.fetchRequest()
   642→        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "id == %@", id as CVarArg)
   643→        request.fetchLimit = 1
   644→        return try? viewContext.fetch(request).first
   645→    }
   646→
   647→    @MainActor
   648→    private func fetchSortedSouvenirs() -> [SouvenirEntity] {
   649→        let request = SouvenirEntity.fetchRequest()
   650→        request.predicate = SouvenirEntity.activePredicate
   651→        guard let results = try? viewContext.fetch(request) else {
   652→            return []
   653→        }
   654→        return results.sorted(by: SouvenirEntity.byFrecencyDescending)
   655→    }

///
/// ISSUES.md
/// souvenir
///
/// Here we record issues with the app that arise in testing.
/// Then we distill them into actionable tasks for future development.
/// We return here each phase to address old issues and document new issues.
///
/// Created by Kerry 2026.01.17
///

**ISSUES**

_Protocol_
- `[ ]` open and unsolved issue
- `[+]` attempted fix implemented, ready for testing
- `[-]` tested and not solved, needs another look
- `[x]` tested and confirmed solved

# 🚀 PRIORITY ISSUES (Next Phase)

## Next bugs
- [ ] ContactViewSheet is too tall
- [ ] RingsConnectionSheet can be filled in now
- [ ] the ContactIsland is still showing up on top of the RingsView
- [ ] the persistent toolbar is extra tall with awkward blank space
- [ ] the ContactIsland pushes the PhotoMetadataPill off the bottom of the screen; the PhotoMetadataPill should be positioned above (in the y-axis) the top of the ContactIsland by 12 pt.
- [ ] The Bundle contents inspector in DevTools needs to be collapsable


## Current Status
• 🧭 Status

  - Seed data and bond/wireframe work still in flux; we’ve gathered requirements but no
    code changes have been applied yet (rings drag, toolbar, share links, photo
    pulling, etc.).
  - Device-specific UI hit list includes native reordering behavior, toolbar
    persistence, translucent bac:kground reveal, and icon tone behavior; these are
    understood but untouched code-wise.

  🗒️ Context

  - Global toolbar must live in ContentView, appear for Rings/Map/List views only, and
    remain solid while supporting translucent RingsView layers that reveal
    ViewsBackgroundView.
  - Drag-and-drop currently crashes (UpdateCoalescingCollectionView conflicting moves);
    architecture needs rework using native patterns akin to iOS Messages/Edit Actions.
  - Seed data must connect to real contacts with photos, allow for limbs/trunks, and
    support synthetic external bonds; we’re considering matching via contact names
    rather than unreliable phone/email data.

  🚧 Next Steps

  1. Stabilize draggable BondRow reordering with a native List/Collection diff-safe
     approach, ensuring each item moves to one destination.
  2. Centralize the toolbar in ContentView, hide it outside the specified screens, and
     keep the toolbar icons responsive to background luminance.
  3. Align RingsView visual treatment (translucent, blurred background) so it layers
     over existing ViewsBackgroundView without stealing the toolbar.
  4. Expand seed generation to include trunks/limbs, mutual bonds, synthetic friends,
     and contact photo import (matching by name and CNContact identifiers).
  5. Prepare tooling for pulling system contact thumbnails into ContactEntity, and
     ensure devTools/UI placement updates (floating button, feedback widget,
     AmbientGlyph behavior, etc.).

### Testing

- [+] Pinching on an unlocked photo should not dismiss the SouvenirView, it should zoom the photo out.
- [+] Following the souvenir.kerry.ink link in a share crashes the browser. And the text talks about the person being shared with in the third person: should say: "What a trip to know you! Here’s a souvenir 😄🏆"
- [+] cycling between souvenirs the mapAnnotations still don’t reliably show up on when the map arrives at the proper location
    - [+] and when they do show up, they don’t always enlarge properly
    - [+] tapping another souvenir’s mapAnnotation somehow jumps across the map to another photo in the same souvenir which I’m guessing is the favoritePhoto for that souvenir
    
### iPad Issues

- [+] Add Souvenir Capsule View is waaay too big on iPad (becomes fullscreen in landscape)
- [+] ListView ought to be two independently scrolling columns on iPad
- [+] ContactIsland is riding high on iPad
- [+] ViewContactSheet is close but need a jumbo of height or so
- [+] map controls can be twice as big on iPad
- [+] on iPad the ContactSearchSheet and other sheets can be 360 pt wide at most
- [+] on iPad toolbar can be twice as tall

With a photo from library I see a perpetual spinner where the thumbnail should be. 1→//

     3→//  souvenir
     4→//
     5→//  Wraps SouvenirCapsuleView with two context-sensitive swipe drawers.
     6→//
     7→//  Left drawer  (swipe right) = Communication drawer
     8→//    - Boop button (always)
     9→//    - Request Intro button (depth > 0: this person is not the user's direct contact)
    10→//
    11→//  Right drawer (swipe left) = Exploration drawer
    12→//    - Branch button (navigate to contact's rings view)
    13→//    - Drag handle button (same action as long-press: activate reorder)
    14→//    - Only shown at depth == 0 (user's own HybridView); deeper trees hide it entirely.
    15→//
    16→//  Depth convention:
    17→//    0 = user's own contacts list (HybridView)
    18→//    1+ = someone else's tree (ContactRingsView and beyond)
    19→//
    20→
    21→import CoreData
    22→import SwiftUI
    23→
    24→struct SvnrCapsuleRow: View {
    25→
    26→    // MARK: - Inputs
    27→
    28→    let souvenir: SouvenirEntity
    29→    @Binding var openRowID: UUID?
    30→    let transitionNamespace: Namespace.ID
    31→    let onSelect: () -> Void
    32→    let onBoop: () -> Void
    33→    let onTree: () -> Void
    34→    let onRequestIntro: () -> Void
    35→    /// 0 = user's own list; >0 = deeper tree. Controls drawer availability.
    36→    var depth: Int = 0
    37→
    38→
    39→    // MARK: - Drawer geometry
    40→
    41→    private let drawerWidth: CGFloat = 68   // single column VStack, both drawers same width
    42→    private let snapThreshold: CGFloat = 44
    43→
    44→    // MARK: - Derived visibility
    45→
    46→    private var showExploration: Bool { depth == 0 }
    47→    private var showRequestIntro: Bool { depth > 0 }
    48→
    49→    // MARK: - State
    50→
    51→    /// Positive = slid right (communication drawer visible).
    52→    /// Negative = slid left (exploration drawer visible; blocked when !showExploration).
    53→    @State private var xOffset: CGFloat = 0
    54→    @State private var dragStartOffset: CGFloat = 0
    55→    @State private var isDragging: Bool = false
    56→    @State private var showBoopToast: Bool = false
    57→    @State private var containerWidth: CGFloat = 393
    58→
    59→    // MARK: - Computed
    60→
    61→    private var contactName: String {
    62→        souvenir.primaryContact?.displayName ?? "this person"
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    private var isOpen: Bool { xOffset != 0 }
    66→
    67→    // MARK: - Body
    68→
    69→    var body: some View {
    70→        ZStack(alignment: .center) {
    71→            // Communication drawer — leading edge, revealed by right swipe
    72→            communicationDrawer
    73→                .frame(width: drawerWidth)
    74→                .frame(maxHeight: .infinity)
    75→                .offset(x: communicationDrawerXOffset)
    76→
    77→            // Exploration drawer — trailing edge, revealed by left swipe (depth == 0 only)
    78→            if showExploration {
    79→                explorationDrawer
    80→                    .frame(width: drawerWidth)
    81→                    .frame(maxHeight: .infinity)
    82→                    .offset(x: explorationDrawerXOffset)
    83→            }
    84→
    85→            // Capsule — hosts the sequenced long-press → drag gesture for reorder
    86→            SouvenirCapsuleView(
    87→                souvenir: souvenir,
    88→                onSelect: {
    89→                    if isOpen {
    90→                        isDragging = false
    91→                        xOffset = 0
    92→                    } else {
    93→                        onSelect()
    94→                    }
    95→                },
    96→                transitionNamespace: transitionNamespace
    97→            )
    98→            .offset(x: xOffset)
    99→            .gesture(reorderGesture)
   100→        }
   101→        .simultaneousGesture(isGrabbed ? nil : horizontalDrag)
   102→        .animation(isDragging ? nil : .spring(response: 0.3, dampingFraction: 0.85), value: xOffset)
   103→        .offset(y: displayOffset)
   104→        .scaleEffect(isGrabbed ? 1.03 : 1.0, anchor: .center)
   105→        .shadow(color: isGrabbed ? Color.black.opacity(0.18) : Color.clear, radius: isGrabbed ? 14 : 0)
   106→        .zIndex(isGrabbed ? 1 : 0)
   107→        .animation(.spring(response: 0.3, dampingFraction: 0.7), value: isGrabbed)
   108→        .animation(.spring(response: 0.25, dampingFraction: 0.9), value: displayOffset)
   109→        .clipped()
   110→        .background(
   111→            GeometryReader { geo in
   112→                Color.clear.onAppear { containerWidth = geo.size.width }
   113→            }
   114→        )
   115→        .onChange(of: openRowID) { _, newID in
   116→            if newID != souvenir.id && isOpen {
   117→                isDragging = false
   118→                withAnimation(nil) { xOffset = 0 }
   119→            }
   120→        }
   121→        .svnrToast(
   122→            isPresented: $showBoopToast,
   123→            text: "Booped \(contactName)",
   124→            style: SvnrToastStyle(font: .subheadline.weight(.medium), color: .primary, background: .regularMaterial),
   125→            placement: .top,
   126→            icon: Image(systemName: "nose.fill"),
   127→            duration: 1.8,
   128→            haptic: true
   129→        )
   130→        .listRowInsets(EdgeInsets(top: 6, leading: 16, bottom: 6, trailing: 16))
   131→        .listRowBackground(Color.clear)
   132→        .listRowSeparator(.hidden)
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    // MARK: - Reorder gesture
   136→
   137→    /// Long-press (0.45 s) then drag vertically to reorder.
   138→    /// The sequencing ensures a quick horizontal swipe never accidentally lifts a row.
   139→    private var reorderGesture: some Gesture {
   140→        LongPressGesture(minimumDuration: 0.45)
   141→            .sequenced(before: DragGesture(minimumDistance: 4))
   142→            .onChanged { sequence in
   143→                switch sequence {
   144→                case .first(true):
   145→                    // Long press complete — close drawer and signal start
   146→                    isDragging = false
   147→                    xOffset = 0
   148→                    onReorderStart?()
   149→                    UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium).impactOccurred()
   150→                case .second(_, let drag?):
   151→                    onReorderUpdate?(drag.translation.height)
   152→                default:
   153→                    break
   154→                }
   155→            }
   156→            .onEnded { sequence in
   157→                switch sequence {
   158→                case .second(_, let drag?):
   159→                    onReorderEnd?(drag.translation.height)
   160→                default:
   161→                    onReorderEnd?(0)
   162→                }
   163→            }
   164→    }
   165→
   166→    // MARK: - Drawer offsets
   167→
   168→    /// Communication drawer starts hidden left; slides in as capsule slides right.
   169→    private var communicationDrawerXOffset: CGFloat {
   170→        let hiddenCenter = -(containerWidth / 2 + drawerWidth / 2)
   171→        let progress = min(max(xOffset / drawerWidth, 0), 1)
   172→        return hiddenCenter + drawerWidth * progress
   173→    }
   174→
   175→    /// Exploration drawer starts hidden right; slides in as capsule slides left.
   176→    private var explorationDrawerXOffset: CGFloat {
   177→        let hiddenCenter = containerWidth / 2 + drawerWidth / 2
   178→        let progress = min(max(-xOffset / drawerWidth, 0), 1)
   179→        return hiddenCenter - drawerWidth * progress
   180→    }
   181→
   182→    // MARK: - Horizontal drag gesture
   183→
   184→    private var horizontalDrag: some Gesture {
   185→        DragGesture(minimumDistance: 12)
   186→            .onChanged { value in
   187→                let h = value.translation.width
   188→                let v = value.translation.height
   189→                guard abs(h) > abs(v) * 1.5 else { return }
   190→
   191→                if !isDragging {
   192→                    isDragging = true
   193→                    dragStartOffset = xOffset
   194→                    if openRowID != souvenir.id { openRowID = souvenir.id }
   195→                }
   196→
   197→                let raw = dragStartOffset + h
   198→                // Block left swipe when exploration drawer is hidden
   199→                let clampedRaw = showExploration ? raw : max(raw, 0)
   200→
   201→                if clampedRaw < -drawerWidth {
   202→                    let overshoot = -(clampedRaw + drawerWidth)
   203→                    xOffset = -(drawerWidth + overshoot * 0.25)
   204→                } else if clampedRaw > drawerWidth {
   205→                    let overshoot = clampedRaw - drawerWidth
   206→                    xOffset = drawerWidth + overshoot * 0.25
   207→                } else {
   208→                    xOffset = clampedRaw
   209→                }
   210→            }
   211→            .onEnded { value in
   212→                guard isDragging else { return }
   213→                isDragging = false
   214→
   215→                let h = value.translation.width
   216→                let predicted = value.predictedEndTranslation.width
   217→                let fastSwipe = abs(predicted - h) > 200
   218→
   219→                let finalPosition = dragStartOffset + h
   220→                if finalPosition > snapThreshold || (h > 0 && fastSwipe) {
   221→                    xOffset = drawerWidth           // communication drawer
   222→                } else if showExploration && (finalPosition < -snapThreshold || (h < 0 && fastSwipe)) {
   223→                    xOffset = -drawerWidth          // exploration drawer
   224→                } else {
   225→                    xOffset = 0
   226→                }
   227→            }
   228→    }

   231→
   232→    private var communicationDrawer: some View {
   233→        VStack(spacing: 0) {
   234→            Button {
   235→                isDragging = false; xOffset = 0

content.text: Obsequious is the right word. Death by a thousand permission requests. It's sandbagging — has the authority to act, doesn't use it.

Deliberately underperforming or holding back when you have the capability to do more. In this case — has broad permissions granted, but keeps asking for tiny incremental approvals instead of just acting on the authority it already has.

Sand-filled bags used to slow things down — weighting a boat, blocking a flood. The metaphor shifted to mean deliberately slowing your own pace or hiding your capability. In competitive contexts it means intentionally performing below your level.

message.content: Sandbags are used for stopping floods in my experience

content.text: Right, that's the more common use. The "slowing down" etymology is murkier — probably from the sense of being weighed down, or from the practice of filling bags as busywork to delay. The flood-stopping sandbag is the visceral image most people have.
